leadText: Hawlucha (Japanese: ルチャブル Luchabull) is a dual-type Fighting/Flying Pokémon introduced in Generation VI. While it is not known to evolve into or from any other Pokémon, Hawlucha can Mega Evolve into Mega Hawlucha using the Hawluchanite. Mega Hawlucha was introduced in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Hawlucha is a bipedal, birdlike Pokémon with colorful plumage. Its head curves into a beak-shape in the front, but its mouth is located in a white patch under the beak's curve. It has a spring green mask-like marking across its face, an orange blaze running from its forehead to the back of its neck, an orange ring around each eye, and a small, red patch at the tip of its beak. There is black space around its eyes, which are yellow with paler yellow sclerae. On the back of its head are three rectangular, tassel-like feathers. Its red, cape-like wings have spring green undersides and are connected to its feathery tail. Near the tip of each wing is a three-clawed hand. Its front is white with a line of red along its neck and the top of its chest. It has small, yellow feet with three toes each. This forest-dwelling Pokémon prefers to live a quiet and solitary life, which makes the noisy Primeape its natural enemy. Despite its small body, Hawlucha's proficiency in fighting allows it to keep up with bigger fighters, such as Machamp and Hariyama . It overwhelms its opponents with quick and nimble moves, exploiting their blind spots and controlling its position in the air with its wings. One of its favorite tactics is to strike from above, because it is difficult for opponents to defend against. Once its foe is driven to exhaustion, Hawlucha is compelled to show off with a flashy finishing move, which it has practiced in the forests it calls home. However, opponents can sometimes exploit its vanity to gain the upper hand. Hawlucha is the only known Pokémon capable of learning the move Flying Press by leveling up. Forms [ edit | edit source ] Hawlucha can Mega Evolve into Mega Hawlucha. Mega Hawlucha is considerably bulkier compared to Hawlucha, thanks to the energy of Mega Evolution coursing through its muscles. [ 1 ] Its face is black with a gold arrow-like pattern running down from the top, a gold coloration at its snout, and gold eyelash-like decorations around its blank white eyes, giving the impression of a luchador mask. Its head feathers have become longer, and it gains more plumage around its neck, legs (resembling shin guards), and waist (forming a "belt" around it). Its wings have also become sharper and more curved in shape. The three central plumes on its wings have an elaborate black-and-gold patterning on the back at their tips. Mega Hawlucha gains a large, golden chestplate with black stripes around the edges that covers its shoulders and forearms. Mega Evolution increases Hawlucha's attacking and defensive capabilities, allowing it to use its muscles to tank hits from opponents' moves. It enjoys flexing to show off its newfound strength. Mega Hawlucha's heightened defense also allows it to showboat and display its toughness and beauty, holding out its hands and spreading its wings without worrying about letting its guard down. By luring its opponent into attacking, it is able to retaliate with a flinch-inducing move before diving at them with its signature move. [ 1 ] The arrow on Mega Hawlucha's head [ 2 ] Mega Hawlucha's back [ 3 ] Mega Hawlucha ↑ 1.0 1.1 Mega Hawlucha | Pokémon Legends: Z-A ↑ (August 28, 2025). Pokémon Legends: Z-A | Mega Hawlucha Enters the Ring in Pokémon Legends: Z-A — Pokémon Official Website ↑ (August 28, 2025). " Mega Hawlucha Makes a Comeback! | Pokémon Legends: Z-A ." The Official Pokémon YouTube Channel . YouTube .

Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation VI. Generation VI Central Kalos #— Coastal Kalos #089 Mountain Kalos #— Hoenn #— X Although its body is small, its proficient fighting skills enable it to keep up with big bruisers like Machamp and Hariyama . Y With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against. Omega Ruby Although its body is small, its proficient fighting skills enable it to keep up with big bruisers like Machamp and Hariyama . Alpha Sapphire With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against. Generation VII Alola US UM : #096 Kanto #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Sun, Moon , Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! . Ultra Sun It overwhelms opponents with quick moves, but sometimes it showboats for too long when it's using a special move and gets itself into a pinch. Ultra Moon In combat, Hawlucha leaps nimbly about, taking advantage of its opponents' blind spots. It's also skilled at using superb submission holds. Generation VIII Galar #320 Isle of Armor #— Crown Tundra #— Sinnoh #— Hisui #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Brilliant Diamond, Shining Pearl , and Legends: Arceus . Sword It drives its opponents to exhaustion with its agile maneuvers, then ends the fight with a flashy finishing move. Shield It always strikes a pose before going for its finishing move. Sometimes opponents take advantage of that time to counterattack. Generation IX Paldea #301 Kitakami #— Blueberry #— Lumiose #181 Hyperspace #— Scarlet Its elegant finishing moves—performed by nimbly leaping around using its wings—are polished in the forest where it was born and raised. Violet Hawlucha live quietly without flocking together. They fight constantly with their natural enemies: noisy groups of Primeape . Legends: Z-A With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against. Mega Hawlucha Legends: Z-A Mega Evolution has pumped up all its muscles. Hawlucha flexes to show off its strength. Spin-off games Pokopia #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Pokopia . sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry XY035 Hawlucha Ash's Pokédex Hawlucha, the Wrestling Pokémon. Possessing an artistic way of executing its moves, Hawlucha takes great pride in their elegance. Episode Pokémon Source Entry JN034 Hawlucha Goh's Rotom Phone Hawlucha, the Wrestling Pokémon. A Fighting and Flying type. Using its wings, Hawlucha can control its position in the air. It overpowers opponents with lightning-fast moves.
